sglang.title: Support penalty in overlap mode; return logprob with chunked prefill; improve benchmark scripts
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Support penalty in overlap mode
Support chunked prefill + input logprob
Improve benchmark script and profiler
rename "token_ids" to "output_ids" in the return value when using --skip-tokenizer-init

Co-authored-by: SangBin Cho <rkooo567@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: dhou-xai <dhou@x.ai>

sglang.title: Reasoning parser
sglang.author: xihuai18
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Based on #3202 and #3859, I fixed some bugs, simplified the implementations and added new features.

Only support --reasoning-parser server argument now. If users want to separate reasoning content, they must specify this option. The parser is chosen only based on this option, but not the model name

vllm.title: [Build] Make sure local main branch is synced when VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We currently use git merge-base main dev to find the base commit of the dev branch, and use the commit to determine which prebuilt wheel to use when installing vLLM without compilation. However, the base commit may not be up-to-date if the local main branch is out dated. Since we cannot sync the for

vllm.title: [V1] Refactor parallel sampling support
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The initial implementation in #10980 went to great efforts to add parallel sampling as a wrapper at the highest layer of abstraction possible. This resulted in a lot of tricky code to post-process RequestOutputs to aggregate them where necessary.
Instead, it probably makes sense to implement paralle

vllm.title: Improve the docs for `TransformersModel`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Improve the docs for TransformersModel to:

Improve its visibility
More clearly explain what it supports
More clearly explain what a remote model writer needs to include

vllm.title: [ROCm] Faster Custom Paged Attention kernels
vllm.author: tjtanaa
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Description
This PR implements a faster Custom Paged Attention (CPA) kernel based on mfma16x16x16 instructions.
This feature is from ROCm/vllm (ROCm#372).
End-to-End Performance gain
Model: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct
Tensor Parallelism: 1
GPU: MI300X



CPA Version
Input length
Output length
KV-cache-dt

vllm.title: Fix `head_dim` not existing in all model configs (Transformers backend)
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Uses getters for:

vocab_size
num_heads
head_size
num_kv_heads

To be more robust to the different keys that different configs use for these values.
Fixes #14139.

vllm.title: [V0][Metrics] Remove unimplemented `vllm:tokens_total`
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It looks like #4464 intended to add this alongside the vllm:iteration_tokens_total histogram, but didn't actually hook it up and would never have appeared in /metrics.
Since it's clearly not critical to anyone, let's just remove it until we hear of a need for it.

vllm.title: [V0][Metrics] Deprecate some KV/prefix cache metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vllm:num_requests_swapped, vllm:cpu_cache_usage_perc and vllm:cpu_prefix_cache_hit_rate will no longer be relevant in V1 since we no longer implement KV cache offloading. So these metrics should be considered deprecated.
And as agreed in #12592, we have added prefix_cache_queries and prefix_cache_hi

vllm.title: [V1] Simplify stats logging
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently the core engine loop wakes up every 2.5 seconds when idle to return scheduler stats to the client, but these won't have changed if there are no unfinished requests.
Update the StatsLogger interface to have record() and log() methods and wire the latter to the existing stats logging task in

vllm.title: [WIP][[V1][Metrics] Implement max_num_generation_tokens,  request_params_n, and request_params_max_tokens metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Part  of #10582
max_num_generation_tokens:

This metric tracks the maximum of num_generation_tokens across a set of identical requests under a parallel sampling parent.
It is the last remaining metric used by the example Grafana dashboard that makes sense in V1.
Add some additional tracking of child

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Allow shared_experts skip quantization for DeepSeekV2/V3
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Forgot to pipe the prefix down to DeepseekV2MLP for shared_experts

vllm.title: [Kernel] Optimize moe intermediate_cache usage
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I'm running into OOM issues at long sequence lengths with deepseek r1, so exploring options here (see o3-mini chat). First I tried moving the silu+mul to be an inplace operation via a new kernel torch.ops._C.silu_and_mul_inplace(intermediate_cache1.view(-1, N)), but it seems easier to reuse memory f

vllm.title: [Docs] Add GPTQModel

vllm.title: [v1] Add comments to the new ragged paged attention Pallas kernel
vllm.author: vanbasten23
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR intends to resolve remaining comments in my previous PR #13379.

vllm.title: [Model] Add support for GraniteMoeShared models
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adds support for the granitemoeshared model type which is based on granitemoe but with the addition of a shared experts layer. A preview model with this architecture can be found at ibm-research/moe-7b-1b-active-shared-experts.
transformers support for this GraniteMoeShared model was recently merged

vllm.title: [core] moe fp8 block quant tuning support
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is required for tuning models with fp8 block quantization (e.g. DeepSeekV3).
The current implementation of fp8 tuning does not take into account the block quantization and hence, when tuning DSv3, it does not factor in the weight scales. This PR adds this support.

(Along with condition to c

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove lru_cache in NvmlCudaPlatform
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The lru_cache mis-caches certain get_device_capability functions and results in errors in the RayExecutor. Since removing lru_cache won't cause any other issue and doesn't affect the server launch time, this PR removes it.
Launch time for Llama-3-8B-FP8 with TP=1 on L4 GPU (the model is pre-download

vllm.title: [core] Pass all driver env vars to ray workers unless excluded
vllm.author: ruisearch42
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently many driver env vars are not passed to ray workers while they should have been. This has caused bugs and confusions.
To fix this, we are passing all driver env vars to ray workers unless excluded, based on the following consideration:

Many driver env vars are intended to pass through to t

vllm.title: Use math.prod instead of np.prod for trivial ops
vllm.author: zhanwenchen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Use Python 3.8 math.prod for lighter imports and overheads. See https://docs.python.org/3/library/math.html#math.prod
FIX #14144

vllm.title: Fix benchmark_moe.py tuning for CUDA devices
vllm.author: mgoin

This appears to have been broken since #12049
Before this PR the tuning would fail when trying to grab CUDA device context
python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_moe.py --tune
